name: nullsec-pineapple-suite
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``nullsec-pineapple-suite`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: git clone https://github.com/bad-antics/nullsec-pineapple-suite && cd nullsec-pineapple-suite && ./install.sh  (Pineapple-side installer; per-payload execution via Pineapple payload menu; restore.sh for factory-reset recovery)

examples.command: git clone https://github.com/bad-antics/nullsec-pineapple-suite && cd nullsec-pineapple-suite && ./install.sh  (Pineapple-side installer; per-payload execution via Pineapple payload menu; restore.sh for factory-reset recovery)

dependencies: WiFi Pineapple Pager firmware 1.0+ + external monitor-mode adapter (Hak5 MK7AC)
algorithms: client device tracking across networks over time (ClientTracker)
produces: recon survey logs and per-payload reports (formats unspecified in README; underlying tools emit airodump-ng CSV/pcap and hcxdumptool pcap)
scheduled task entries and IDS logs on the Pineapple
novelty: None algorithmically — these are ~150-line shell wrappers orchestrating airodump-ng, hcxdumptool, reaver, and nmap. The only non-trivial original work is Pineapple-platform orchestration: a shared interface-autodetection lib (lib/nullsec-iface.sh), curated Pineapple-specific loot/log path layout, and a couple of higher-level composite workflows (temporal WiFiTimeline activity mapping, SocialMapper proximity-graphing, DroneHunter) that combine baseline tools rather than implement new recon math.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
